mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and boosting corporate earnings expectations, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where the FEDFUNDS rate has been below 2% for at least 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits and consumer confidence decline over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous and sustained declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling an impending economic slowdown and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where both RSAFS 3-month % change is below -1% and HOUST 3-month % change is below -5%.

mechanism_narrative: A strong and consistent rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following a period of contraction indicates a recovery in the supply side of the economy, boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, particularly during post-recession periods.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO 1-month % change is greater than 0.5% within a declared post-recession recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 35% of periods where the CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the longer term of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 60-day forward returns show a statistically insignificant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window, or are negative in > 40% of periods with sustained PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based weakening of aggregate demand, signaling a likely recession and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances where GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth is below 1.0% and RSAFS 3-month % change is below -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is actively increasing, it signals aggressive monetary tightening, which typically leads to short-term negative pressure on equity markets as discount rates rise, impacting S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: aggressive monetary tightening with high inflation
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 30% of periods where FEDFUNDS has increased by over 0.25% in the last month and CPIAUCSL 12-month % change is above 3.0%.
